score_injection_action: Rank=Disgraced with close_fail_qa_pass(-12) in recent events; this run applies zero rubber-stamping on OBJ fixes: each fix is verified by reading the actual ticket text and tracing residual contradictions — 2 residual defects found that BA missed when applying fixes.

qa.overall_assessment: BA resolved 4 of 5 objections cleanly. OBJ-3 is partially fixed: AC10 itself is correct, but the fix left 2 residual locations in the same ticket with the incorrect 'mirroring blast-radius-tool.py:35' attribution (Requirements Decomposition table R10 and Reference Source / Dev Constraints line). These are active misleading instructions to DEV. Additionally, OBJ-4's investigation contains a factual inaccuracy: BA states that dev-overnight.md 'lines 248-263 enumerate agent types literally' but those lines only show 2 example entries (architect, ba) plus an ellipsis comment — they are template instructions, not a comprehensive enumeration. The actual enumeration is driven exclusively by prompt-workflow.py agent_types, which is already covered by AC5. This inaccuracy risks the DEV agent wasting effort looking for a literal list that does not exist or making an incorrect change.

objections.title: AC2 fail gates removed (RESOLVED)
objections.description: AC2 THEN clause now reads: 'agents/ba.md contains the 6-step Reference Resolution procedure AND the list of implicit reference trigger words'. The NOTE clause explicitly states 'The three new BA-QA fail gates belong exclusively in agents/qa.md (see AC3); they MUST NOT be duplicated in agents/ba.md'. Fix is complete and correct.
objections.evidence: Verified: ticket line 142-148 shows AC2 THEN clause without fail gates + NOTE clause explicitly directing fail gates to AC3 only.

objections.title: AC5 third registration site added (RESOLVED)
objections.description: AC5 now explicitly requires 'graphify' in all three sets: CP_AGENTS (pretool-cp-checkin.py:~72), ALLOWED_AGENTS (scripts/spec-check.py:~59), and agent_types list (hooks/prompt-workflow.py:598-603). The AND clause explains the sentinel-file creation consequence. Fix is complete and correct.
objections.evidence: Verified: ticket lines 162-169 show AC5 GIVEN clause includes all three files; THEN clause requires 'graphify' in all three sets; AND clause verifies sentinel creation. Comment documents the OBJ-2 fix rationale and test-writer precedent.

objections.title: OBJ-3 PARTIALLY FIXED: AC10 corrected but 2 residual 'mirroring blast-radius-tool.py:35' locations remain active in ticket
objections.description: BA fixed AC10 itself (lines 196-202): the THEN clause now correctly cites 'spec Section 5 Risk item 7 and arch-8' as authority for sensitive-data patterns, and 'arch-8' for noise patterns. AC10 is correct. However, the fix left two other locations in the SAME ticket document that still contain the incorrect attribution: (1) Requirements Decomposition table R10 (ticket line 114): 'graphify_lib.py defines EXCLUDE_FRAGMENTS tuple (mirroring blast-radius-tool.py:35)' — this is the exact false attribution that OBJ-3 flagged, still present verbatim; (2) Reference Source / Dev Constraints (ticket line 53): 'Arch-8 MANDATES sensitive data exclusion enforced in graphify_lib.py using EXCLUDE_FRAGMENTS pattern (mirroring blast-radius-tool.py:35)' — same false attribution, still present. DEV agents reading these sections will receive contradictory instructions: AC10 says 'do NOT mirror blast-radius-tool.py:35 for sensitive data' while the Requirements Decomposition table and Dev Constraints say exactly the opposite. This is a blocking defect that will cause implementation confusion.
affected_locations: ticket line 114: Requirements Decomposition R10 acceptance_criterion column
ticket line 53: Reference Source section Dev constraint for arch-8
objections.evidence: Read ticket line 114: '| R10 | ... | graphify_lib.py defines EXCLUDE_FRAGMENTS tuple (mirroring blast-radius-tool.py:35) |' — false attribution verbatim. Read ticket line 53: 'Arch-8 MANDATES sensitive data exclusion enforced in graphify_lib.py using EXCLUDE_FRAGMENTS pattern (mirroring blast-radius-tool.py:35).' — same false attribution. Confirmed blast-radius-tool.py:35-38: EXCLUDE_FRAGMENTS = ('/venv/', '/worktrees/', '/.archive/', '/plugins/', '/.git/', '/__pycache__/', '/node_modules/') — NO .env/credentials/keys/logs. The context JSON R10 acceptance_criterion is correctly fixed but the TICKET document contains the old text at two locations.
objections.required_fix: Update ticket R10 in the Requirements Decomposition table (line 114) to match AC10: remove 'mirroring blast-radius-tool.py:35' and replace with 'covering .env, credentials, keys, logs (from spec §5 Risk-7 + arch-8) and filesystem noise (.git, venv, __pycache__, node_modules)'. Update the Dev Constraints line 53 to remove the false 'mirroring blast-radius-tool.py:35' attribution for arch-8 sensitive-data exclusion.

objections.title: OBJ-4 SUBSTANTIALLY FIXED with one factual inaccuracy in the investigation
objections.description: commands/dev-overnight.md has been added to the PR2 affected-files list (ticket line 251 and context JSON files_to_modify — confirmed). The OBJ-4 Investigation section documents the rationale for the modification. The fix to include dev-overnight.md is correct. However, the investigation contains a factual inaccuracy: BA states 'the overnight Step 1 explicit Bash form (lines 248-264) also enumerates agent types literally and needs graphify added' — but actual inspection of dev-overnight.md lines 248-264 shows ONLY two example entries (architect.json, ba.json) followed by '... (one Write per agent type)' and '# ... (one printf per agent type)' ellipsis comments. These lines are template instructions with examples, NOT a literal comprehensive enumeration of all agent types. There is no standalone comprehensive list of agent types in dev-overnight.md outside of Form A/B examples. The ACTUAL comprehensive enumeration lives in prompt-workflow.py:598 agent_types list (already addressed by AC5/OBJ-2). The 'add graphify to lines 248-263' instruction in OBJ-4 investigation is therefore misleading — it will cause DEV to add graphify to the example blocks (lines 249-250 / 260-261) which are just template samples, while the actual functional registration is done via prompt-workflow.py. This is a minor inaccuracy in DEV guidance but does not create a missing requirement gap.
objections.evidence: Read dev-overnight.md lines 248-264: lines 249-250 show Write tool examples for architect.json and ba.json, followed by '... (one Write per agent type)' ellipsis. Lines 260-261 show printf examples for architect.json and ba.json, followed by '# ... (one printf per agent type)' ellipsis. No comprehensive agent list enumeration exists in dev-overnight.md at these lines. Confirmed: context JSON files_to_modify includes 'commands/dev-overnight.md' — the file is correctly scoped.
objections.required_fix: Clarify the OBJ-4 investigation: the lines 248-263 modification is to ADD graphify.json examples to the Form A/Form B template code blocks (so the examples are complete and accurate), not to maintain a literal master enumeration. The master enumeration is in prompt-workflow.py. The current instruction is accurate in outcome (add graphify to these example blocks) but inaccurate in framing (they are not 'literal enumerations'). Low severity — DEV will achieve the correct outcome even with the imprecise description.

objections.title: AC12 added covering full Command Coverage table (RESOLVED)
objections.description: AC12 is present (ticket lines 211-221) and covers all 6 entries from the spec Section 5 Command Coverage table: /dev (Steps 1.5 + 7.5 active), /redev (inherits /dev), /pull (post-pull graphify-maintain.py update), /clean (explicitly disabled), /dev-overnight (sentinel fanout includes graphify + global update integration). The clean.md file exists at commands/clean.md (confirmed). Fix is complete and correct.
objections.evidence: Verified: ticket AC12 GIVEN clause lists commands/dev.md, commands/dev-overnight.md, commands/redev.md, commands/pull.md, commands/clean.md. THEN clauses cover all 5 command entries plus /pull. Context JSON success_criteria includes AC12 covering the full table.

new_objections.title: R10 in Requirements Decomposition table still carries false 'mirroring blast-radius-tool.py:35' attribution
new_objections.description: This is a sub-finding of the OBJ-3 partial fix. The Requirements Decomposition table R10 acceptance_criterion column (ticket line 114) reads: 'graphify_lib.py defines EXCLUDE_FRAGMENTS tuple (mirroring blast-radius-tool.py:35)'. This contradicts the corrected AC10 and the corrected context JSON R10. DEV will read this table as an implementation contract alongside the ACs. The false attribution in R10 creates a direct contradiction with AC10's corrected text.
new_objections.evidence: Ticket line 114: grep result '| R10 | ... | graphify_lib.py defines EXCLUDE_FRAGMENTS tuple (mirroring blast-radius-tool.py:35) |'
new_objections.required_fix: Update R10 acceptance_criterion in the Requirements Decomposition table to: 'graphify_lib.py defines EXCLUDE_FRAGMENTS tuple covering .env, credentials, keys, logs (spec §5 Risk-7 + arch-8) and filesystem noise patterns (.git, venv, __pycache__, node_modules per arch-8); does NOT mirror blast-radius-tool.py:35 which lacks sensitive-data patterns'

new_objections.title: Dev Constraints in Reference Source section still says 'arch-8 MANDATES EXCLUDE_FRAGMENTS pattern (mirroring blast-radius-tool.py:35)'
new_objections.description: Ticket line 53 (Reference Source / Dev Constraints): 'Arch-8 MANDATES sensitive data exclusion enforced in graphify_lib.py using EXCLUDE_FRAGMENTS pattern (mirroring blast-radius-tool.py:35)'. This is the MANDATORY instruction to DEV. DEV reads the Reference Source / Dev Constraints section before the AC-level details. This is the highest-priority contradictory text. It will override or confuse the corrected AC10 because it appears under the authoritative 'Dev constraint' heading.
new_objections.evidence: Ticket line 53, confirmed by sed -n '53p': 'Arch-8 MANDATES sensitive data exclusion enforced in graphify_lib.py using EXCLUDE_FRAGMENTS pattern (mirroring blast-radius-tool.py:35).'
new_objections.required_fix: Update ticket line 53 Dev constraint for arch-8 to: 'Arch-8 MANDATES sensitive data exclusion enforced in graphify_lib.py using EXCLUDE_FRAGMENTS pattern including .env, credentials, keys, logs (spec §5 Risk-7) — NOT mirroring blast-radius-tool.py:35 which is for filesystem noise only, not sensitive-data exclusion'

evidence_quality.notes: All 5 OBJ fix claims are evidence-backed. BA cited specific file/line evidence for each fix. OBJ-3 fix evidence is accurate for AC10 but missed the two residual locations. OBJ-4 investigation evidence is accurate for the file inclusion but partially inaccurate in characterizing lines 248-263 as 'literal enumeration'. Overall: factual claims are substantiated; the residual defects are errors of omission in the fix scope rather than unsupported assertions.

scope_alignment.notes: OBJ-1 resolved. OBJ-5 resolved with AC12. No new scope narrowing found. Command Coverage table is now fully covered. AC12 correctly identifies commands/clean.md (which exists at 761 lines, confirmed).

OBJ-4 investigation contained a factual inaccuracy (claiming lines 248-263 'enumerate agents literally' when they only show 2 examples + ellipsis).

all_findings.recommendation: Update R10 table cell to match AC10 corrected text: remove 'mirroring blast-radius-tool.py:35', cite spec §5 Risk-7 + arch-8 for sensitive-data patterns.

all_findings.issue: Dev Constraints still says 'Arch-8 MANDATES sensitive data exclusion enforced in graphify_lib.py using EXCLUDE_FRAGMENTS pattern (mirroring blast-radius-tool.py:35)'. This is the authoritative dev instruction heading. It contradicts the corrected AC10.
all_findings.recommendation: Update arch-8 constraint to specify .env/credentials/keys/logs exclusion per spec §5 Risk-7 + arch-8, explicitly noting that blast-radius-tool.py:35 is NOT the source for these patterns.
